spec: offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-01-from-spool-sprint22-drain-forensics-spec.md (full Sprint 22 spec from Spool post-Drain-6) + offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-01-from-spool-drain-test-5-us216-still-broken-and-wake-on-power-verification.md (drain context Section 1) + offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-042-release-theme-field-broke-us246-247-248-test-stubs.md (US-268 source) + offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-044-test-migration-0005-asserts-literal-last-version-broken-by-v0006.md (US-269 source)
backlogItem: B-043 (Pi auto-sync + conditional-shutdown -- ladder-fires phase did NOT actually fix bug, drain-forensics phase) + TD-042 (US-268) + TD-044 (US-269)

testBaseline.note: Post-Sprint-21 close baseline. main@335630e (V0.21.0). Verify exact count at sprint start; TD-042's 24 broken tests + TD-044's 1 broken test count were pre-existing as of Sprint 21 close per memory.

sprintNotes: GOAL: Drain Test 6 (2026-05-01 21:58-22:19 CDT) was the 6th consecutive hard-crash. US-252's Sprint-21 fix (decouple tick from display loop + add forensic vcell column + 3 stage event_types to power_log) DID NOT fix the underlying bug. Forensic data: 1 row in power_log for the entire 21-min drain (zero STAGE_WARNING/IMMINENT/TRIGGER rows), zero _enterStage lines in journalctl, vcell column present but never populated, hard crash at 22:19:03. CIO selected Spool's Option B (logger + best-guess fix in same sprint). Sprint 22 ships both: US-262 forensic logger captures discriminator data on Drain Test 7; US-265+US-266+US-267 implement all three discriminator fixes additively (don't pick one hypothesis -- ship all three, let Drain 7 + logger CSV name which one mattered).

EXECUTION ORDER (deps-aware): All 8 stories are TIER 1 (zero deps between them). Recommended order: US-262 logger FIRST (gates Drain Test 7 utility), then US-263 boot-reason (independent), US-264 dashboard (independent, touches Sprint-21 US-257 dashboard_layout.py), US-265/266/267 discriminator trio (additive), US-268 TD-042 + US-269 TD-044 (hygiene closures, no overlap with power-mgmt code).

THEME: 'The instrumentation that should have shipped Sprint 21.' US-252 was a best-guess fix without forensic instrumentation to prove it worked. Sprint 22 corrects that pattern: ship the logger so the next drain test produces actionable post-mortem data, then ship all three discriminator hypotheses additively so post-drain we know which (or which combination) was the actual bug. Discriminator truth-table per Spool: pd_tick_count stays 0 -> Story US-265 was the fix; pd_tick_count increments but pd_stage stays NORMAL -> US-266; pd_stage advances but power_log has no STAGE_* rows -> US-267; all three signals present -> all three needed and ladder finally works.

CONSTRAINTS (per feedback_runtime_validation_required.md): the discriminator stories are the textbook case of 'tests-pass alone is insufficient -- live drill is the acceptance.' US-265/266/267 each MUST include a synthetic test that would FAIL against pre-fix code (the standard discriminator gate from Sprint 19 US-234 + US-236). For US-267 specifically the synthetic test must stair-step VCELL through all 3 thresholds AND assert power_log STAGE_* rows exist after the writer call returns (proving fsync flushed before crash window). Live drill (Drain Test 7) remains a POST-SPRINT ACTION ITEM per CIO no-human-tasks rule.

GROUNDING SOURCES: 2026-05-01 Spool sprint22-drain-forensics-spec note (full spec with story shapes + discriminator truth-table) + 2026-05-01 Spool drain-test-5 note (drain history Section 1) + 6 drain tests of empirical hard-crash data + Sprint 21 US-252 completionNotes (the fix that didn't fix it -- documents the audit method that found tick/display coupling but missed that fixing tick-thread alone is necessary-not-sufficient) + TD-042 + TD-044 records.

POST-SPRINT-22 ACTION ITEMS (NOT in sprint.json per CIO rule): (1) CIO + Spool run Drain Test 7 with US-262 logger deployed; expect drain-forensics CSV at /var/log/eclipse-obd/ + populated pd_tick_count + pd_stage columns + STAGE_* rows in power_log if any of US-265/266/267 was the actual fix. (2) Spool reads CSV + power_log post-Drain-7 and applies discriminator truth-table to call verdict on which hypothesis was correct; files inbox note. (3) PM addresses Story 7 from Spool spec (phantom-path drift in sprint.json scope.filesToTouch -- 8-session pattern; PM-side template generator audit); tracked in offices/pm/action-items.md. (4) Marcus bumps deploy/RELEASE_VERSION at sprint close per feedback_pm_sprint_close_version_bump.md (V0.21.0 -> V0.22.0 expected; minor for new diagnostic surface area + actual ladder-firing fix). (5) Stale local sprint branches: 7 accumulated (sprint/data-v2, ops-hardening, runtime-fixes, sprint20-foundation, sprint21-ladder-fires, tuning-safety, wiring) -- CIO call on remote delete.

stories.title: Drain forensics logger -- 14-column CSV every 5s on BATTERY
stories.intent: Drain Test 6 produced 1 power_log row across a 21-minute drain. Without forensic instrumentation we cannot tell whether tick() ran (pd_tick_count), whether the orchestrator's stage state advanced (pd_stage), whether the Pi5 SoC browned out before VCELL hit the threshold (throttled_hex bits 0/16), or whether write-path fsync was the gap (CSV pd_stage advances but power_log stays empty). This story ships the logger that turns Drain Test 7 into actionable data. Activates when PowerSource == BATTERY; writes one CSV row every 5s to /var/log/eclipse-obd/drain-forensics.csv with append-mode + os.fsync(); rotates by timestamp suffix on AC restoration. After this ships, the post-mortem signal is determined by whether US-265/266/267 (the three discriminator fixes) actually engaged.

src/pi/power/orchestrator.py (UPDATE -- expose tickCount counter incremented in tick() + currentStage accessor; both read-only; thread-safe snapshot)

deploy/drain-forensics.service (NEW -- systemd unit invoking scripts/drain_forensics.py with proper user/working-dir/restart-on-failure; only enabled, not started -- the script self-decides start based on BATTERY state)
deploy/drain-forensics.timer (NEW -- systemd timer firing the unit every 5s; OnBootSec=30 OnUnitActiveSec=5 + Persistent=true)

tests/pi/diagnostics/test_drain_forensics_logger.py (NEW -- mocks I2cClient.readWord (MAX17048 regs) + vcgencmd subprocess + PowerDownOrchestrator accessors; asserts CSV row format + 14 columns populated + os.fsync called per row + rotation on AC transition)

groundingRefs.value: Drain Test 6: 1 row in power_log for a 21-min battery-state window; zero STAGE_* rows; vcell column present but never populated
14 CSV columns required; pd_tick_count + throttled_hex are the two diagnostic crown jewels
5-second cadence; append-mode + os.fsync per row so no buffered data lost on hard crash
MAX17048 reg 0x16 CRATE is signed 16-bit big-endian, 0.208 %/hr/LSB; 0xFFFF means disabled (return None)

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg `drain_forensics|tickCount|currentStage|vcgencmd|throttled` src/ tests/ scripts/ deploy/. Record current accessor surface + any conflicts.

scripts/drain_forensics.py: detects PowerSource transition AC->BATTERY and BATTERY->AC; writes CSV row every 5s while on BATTERY; rotates file by timestamp suffix on each AC->BATTERY transition; closes file cleanly on BATTERY->AC
PowerDownOrchestrator exposes thread-safe tickCount + currentStage accessors; tickCount increments inside tick() before any early-return guard

deploy/drain-forensics.service + .timer install via standard pattern (deploy-pi.sh does NOT need modification this sprint -- timer enabled separately via post-deploy hook or manually for now; document in story handoff)

Fast suite + new tests pass; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors
invariants: Logger never crashes the data_logger loop (separate process via systemd; failure of forensics is not failure of capture)
fsync after every CSV row -- buffered data loss on hard crash is unacceptable for this story
tickCount accessor must NOT acquire any lock that tick() itself holds (snapshot read pattern)
Logger no-ops when AC power present (zero overhead during normal operation)

If PowerDownOrchestrator.tick() has no obvious place to add counter without lock contention -- STOP, document and pick simpler counter approach
If systemd-timer scheduling at 5s cadence proves unreliable on Pi5 -- STOP, document and consider a sleep-loop daemon instead

stories.title: Boot-reason detector -- startup_log table + journalctl parse
stories.intent: Right now post-mortem of every drain test requires manual `journalctl --list-boots` inspection to determine whether the prior boot ended with a clean shutdown or a hard crash. This story makes that queryable by writing a startup_log row at every Pi startup with the prior-boot disposition. Drain Test 6 + earlier drain tests would have been instantly classifiable as hard-crash from this table alone; future drains can be validated for graceful-shutdown signature without operator inspection.

filesToTouch: src/pi/diagnostics/boot_reason.py (NEW -- detect boot-id from /proc/sys/kernel/random/boot_id; parse `journalctl --list-boots` for prior boot's last entry timestamp + presence of shutdown record; write startup_log row with prior_boot_clean bool + timestamps)
src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py (UPDATE -- add SCHEMA_STARTUP_LOG table: boot_id TEXT PK, prior_boot_clean INTEGER (0/1), prior_last_entry_ts TEXT, current_boot_first_entry_ts TEXT, recorded_at TEXT)

tests/pi/diagnostics/test_boot_reason.py (NEW -- mocks subprocess.run for journalctl --list-boots returning canned multi-boot output with + without shutdown records; asserts prior_boot_clean correctly inferred for both cases)
filesToRead: src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py (existing table-add pattern; ensure*Column migration helpers)
src/pi/lifecycle.py or src/pi/main.py (where to invoke boot_reason at startup)

groundingRefs.value: journalctl --list-boots returns prior boots with timestamps; presence of shutdown record distinguishes clean vs crash
Manual inspection burden = hours per drain test post-mortem; queryable startup_log eliminates

stories.title: Dashboard VCELL promotion + SOC demotion with uncalibrated annotation
stories.intent: Drain Test 6 dashboard at the moment of failure: SOC=96% while VCELL was approaching the WARNING threshold of 3.70V. SOC is known broken (40+ point drift near depletion -- the reason Sprint 19 US-234 moved trigger source SOC->VCELL). The dashboard currently misleads the operator at the exact moment they need accurate state. This story swaps prominence in the Power card: VCELL becomes the large primary number; SOC becomes a small secondary line annotated `(uncalibrated)`. Stage-based NE-quadrant tinting per US-257 stays unchanged.

stories.title: Discriminator A: verify tick thread starts + health-check assertion
stories.intent: Hypothesis A from Spool's discriminator truth-table: the dedicated _powerDownTickThread daemon (US-252 fix) silently never starts, or starts but dies immediately. If true, US-262 logger's pd_tick_count column will stay at 0 throughout Drain 7. This story proves the thread starts AND keeps running by (1) auditing _powerDownTickThread instantiation in HardwareManager + lifecycle to confirm thread.start() is reached and daemon=True; (2) adding a startup journal log line `PowerDownOrchestrator tick thread started, tid=<id>` so journalctl can confirm; (3) adding a health-check that logs pd_tick_count value to journalctl every 60s and RAISES if non-incrementing. After this ships, pd_tick_count behavior post-Drain-7 is fully diagnosable.

src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py (UPDATE -- audit _powerDownTickThread instantiation path; confirm thread.start() reached + daemon=True; add log line at thread spawn site for symmetry)

tests/pi/hardware/test_power_down_tick_thread_health.py (NEW -- unit test that instantiates HardwareManager with mocked PowerDownOrchestrator whose tick() does NOTHING; runs the health-check; asserts ERROR-level log line + alarm event after one health-check window when on BATTERY; asserts NO alarm when on AC)

stories.title: Discriminator B: audit tick() gating + DEBUG-log early-returns
stories.intent: Hypothesis B from Spool's discriminator truth-table: pd_tick_count increments (thread alive) but pd_stage stays NORMAL even when VCELL crosses thresholds. Cause class: an early-return guard inside tick() silently bails before reaching the stage-comparison logic -- e.g., `if power_source != BATTERY` returning UNKNOWN, `if vcell is None`, threshold comparison with NoneType, stale-cached VCELL value. This story reads tick() end-to-end, identifies every guard, adds a DEBUG log line at each early-return capturing the value that caused the bail, and writes a synthetic test that would FAIL against pre-fix code if any of these guards is silently swallowing the BATTERY path.

filesToTouch: src/pi/power/orchestrator.py (UPDATE -- read tick() end-to-end; for every conditional that can early-return without advancing stage, add `logger.debug('tick early-return: <reason> value=<x>')`; specifically annotate: power_source != BATTERY, vcell is None, threshold comparison with None, stale-cached VCELL value detection; preserve existing behavior + invariants)

src/pi/hardware/ups_monitor.py (READ-ONLY-TOUCH -- if tick() consumes getPowerSource() return UNKNOWN as a quiet skip-condition, add a one-line comment + DEBUG log at the consumer site only, NOT in ups_monitor)

tests/pi/power/test_tick_gating_no_silent_bail.py (NEW -- 4 parametrized tests, each mocks one guard's bail-condition (BATTERY/UNKNOWN, vcell=None, threshold=None, stale-cache); asserts DEBUG log message captures the value that caused the bail; asserts NO stage transition occurs; this test would FAIL pre-fix because zero DEBUG logs existed at early-return sites)
doNotTouch: PowerDownOrchestrator state-machine logic (US-216 + US-234 territory; correct per Spool)

groundingRefs.value: If pd_tick_count increments but pd_stage stays NORMAL when vcell_v < 3.70 -> this discriminator was the bug
Common gating-logic failure modes: stale VCELL cache, getPowerSource() returning UNKNOWN treated as 'not BATTERY', NoneType in threshold comparison
Synthetic test must mock at I2cClient.readWord level (Sprint 19 US-235 + Sprint 20 US-245 pattern); high-level mocking would NOT discriminate the bug

src/pi/power/orchestrator.py: every early-return in tick() emits a DEBUG log line capturing the value that caused the bail; behavior unchanged (same code-paths, same returns)

tests/pi/power/test_tick_gating_no_silent_bail.py: 4 parametrized tests cover BATTERY/UNKNOWN, vcell=None, threshold=None, stale-cache; each asserts DEBUG log captures expected value AND no stage transition fired; this would FAIL pre-fix
Fast suite + new tests pass; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors
invariants: Behavior of tick() unchanged -- only DEBUG logging added; no logic refactored
DEBUG logs do not fire on the happy-path (must be early-return-only)
Synthetic test FAILS against pre-fix code (no DEBUG logs existed); PASSES against post-fix code
stopConditions: If audit reveals tick() has NO guards that can silently bail (single linear path) -- STOP, document; this story's hypothesis would be invalidated and discriminator-B would be ruled out a-priori

If adding logs reveals an actual bug (e.g., stale VCELL cache discovered during audit) -- STOP, file follow-up story; do NOT fix in-place this sprint (the discriminator design depends on additive-only changes)

stories.title: Discriminator C: fsync stage-row writes + audit error propagation
stories.intent: Hypothesis C from Spool's discriminator truth-table: pd_stage advances in CSV (orchestrator state machine fires correctly) but power_log STAGE_* rows never appear -- meaning the write path itself is the gap. SQLite writes can buffer in WAL or commit-but-not-fsync; on hard crash the rows are lost. Additionally, an exception inside power_db.logShutdownStage may be swallowed silently. This story adds explicit conn.commit() + os.fsync(conn.fileno()) (or PRAGMA synchronous=FULL on the write connection) and wraps the INSERT in try/except that LOGs at ERROR level and re-raises -- never swallows. After this ships, the discriminator is conclusive: pd_stage advances + STAGE_* rows present means the ladder finally works.

filesToTouch: src/pi/power/power_db.py (UPDATE -- in logShutdownStage: explicit conn.commit() immediately followed by os.fsync(conn.fileno()) where supported; if SQLite connection does not expose fileno cleanly, set PRAGMA synchronous=FULL on the connection; wrap INSERT in try/except OperationalError + Exception -> log ERROR + re-raise; preserve idempotency)

tests/pi/power/test_logshutdown_stage_fsync_and_error_propagation.py (NEW -- 3 tests: (a) successful logShutdownStage call -> assert os.fsync mock invoked OR PRAGMA synchronous=FULL set; (b) INSERT raises OperationalError -> assert ERROR log + exception re-raised, NOT swallowed; (c) stair-step VCELL test that drives all 3 stage thresholds and asserts power_log has 3 STAGE_* rows AFTER each writer call returns -- the runtime-validation gate)
filesToRead: src/pi/obdii/database.py (SQLite connection setup; PRAGMA + WAL pattern)
tests/pi/power/test_staged_shutdown_actually_fires.py (US-252 stair-step pattern; reuse fixture if possible)

groundingRefs.value: If pd_stage advances but power_log has no STAGE_* rows -> this discriminator was the bug
SQLite buffers in WAL by default; hard crash before fsync loses rows
Synthetic test must stair-step VCELL through all 3 thresholds AND assert STAGE_* rows present AFTER writer returns (proves fsync flushed)

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg `logShutdownStage|fsync|synchronous|commit\(\)` src/pi/power/power_db.py src/pi/obdii/database.py. Confirm no existing fsync on stage writes.
src/pi/power/power_db.py: logShutdownStage explicit conn.commit() + os.fsync (or PRAGMA synchronous=FULL); INSERT wrapped in try/except that logs ERROR and re-raises -- no swallow

tests/pi/power/test_logshutdown_stage_fsync_and_error_propagation.py: 3 tests cover happy-path fsync invocation + error propagation + stair-step end-to-end with rows-present assertion; all 3 would FAIL pre-fix
Fast suite + new tests pass; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors
invariants: logShutdownStage is idempotent (same stage written twice in close succession does not corrupt state)
fsync or synchronous=FULL adds <50ms per write; no measurable impact on tick-loop cadence
Errors are NEVER swallowed; ERROR log + re-raise is the contract
stopConditions: If SQLite connection-level fsync proves unsupported on the deployed sqlite3 version -- STOP, fall back to PRAGMA synchronous=FULL with explicit commit
If wrapping INSERT in try/except surfaces an actual exception class that Sprint 21 was silently swallowing -- STOP, file follow-up + capture the swallowed exception type

stories.title: TD-042 close: add theme field to release-record test stubs
stories.intent: Per TD-042: CIO commit `57bdda6` made `theme` a hard-required key in RELEASE_RECORD_KEYS. The Pi-side update-checker tests (US-247) and server-side release-reader/endpoint tests (US-246) were authored before that commit -- their `_serverRecord` + `_writeLocalDeployVersion` + JSONL fixtures lack `theme`, so validateRelease silently rejects them and 24 tests fail. Mechanical addition: add `theme` to each stub site. No logic changes. Closes a sprint-contract-integrity wound that has been carrying since Sprint 20 close.

stories.intent: Per TD-044: tests/server/test_migration_0005_dtc_log.py:158 asserts `versions[-1] == '0005'`. Recommendation per TD-044 (option a): tighten the assertion to a SemVer-shape regex (`r'^\d{4}$'`) so the next migration doesn't break it again.
